1	/**
2	 * Context Exercise Parser
3	 *
4	 * Parses ContextExtractions LaTeX text into structured exercise segments.
5	 * Shared between the admin ContextExerciseViewer (client) and the
6	 * server-side exercise creation service.
7	 */
8	
9	export interface ParsedExercise {
10	  number: number
11	  title: string
12	  /** The LaTeX header that matched (e.g. "\\textbf{תרגיל 1}") */
13	  header: string
14	  latexContent: string
15	  solution: string | null
16	  /** The solution header if present (e.g. "\\section*{פתרון תרגיל 1}") */
17	  solutionHeader: string | null
18	  hasDiagram: boolean
19	  /** Character offsets within the extraction run text for reconstruction */
20	  startIndex: number
21	  endIndex: number
22	}
23	
24	export interface ParsedSegment {
25	  exercises: ParsedExercise[]
26	  extractionIndex: number
27	  /** Original text of this extraction run */
28	  originalText: string
29	}
30	
31	/** Check if text contains TikZ or minipage diagram markers */
32	export function hasDiagramCheck(text: string): boolean {
33	  return /\\(begin|end)\{(?:tikzpicture|minipage)\}/.test(text)
34	}
35	
36	/**
37	 * Parse LaTeX text into structured exercise segments.
38	 * Handles multiple extraction runs separated by \n\n---\n\n
39	 * Tracks character positions for write-back support.
40	 */
41	export function parseContextText(contextText: string): ParsedSegment[] {
42	  if (!contextText || !contextText.trim()) {
43	    return []
44	  }
45	
46	  // Split by extraction run delimiter
47	  const runs = contextText.split(/\n\n---\n\n/)
48	  const segments: ParsedSegment[] = []
49	
50	  for (let runIndex = 0; runIndex < runs.length; runIndex++) {
51	    const runText = runs[runIndex]
52	    if (!runText.trim()) continue
53	
54	    const exercises: ParsedExercise[] = []
55	
56	    // Pattern to match exercise titles:
57	    //   \textbf{תרגיל N ...} or \section*{תרגיל N ...} or \subsection*{תרגיל N ...}
58	    const exercisePattern =
59	      /(?:\\textbf\{(תרגיל\s+(\d+)[^}]*)\}|\\section\*?\{(תרגיל\s+(\d+)[^}]*)\}|\\subsection\*?\{(תרגיל\s+(\d+)[^}]*)\})/g

62	    // Does NOT require \begin{enumerate} — handles mid-enumerate setcounter too

66	    let match

457	          if (tok[0].startsWith('\\begin{enumerate}')) {
458	            level++
459	            if (level === 1) {
460	              inOrphan = !tok[1]
461	            }
462	          } else if (tok[0] === '\\end{enumerate}') {
463	            if (level === 1) inOrphan = false
464	            level--
465	            if (level < 0) level = 0
466	          } else if (tok[0].startsWith('\\setcounter')) {
467	            if (level === 1) inOrphan = false
468	          } else if (tok[0] === '\\item' && level === 1 && inOrphan) {
469	            orphanItems.push(regionStart + tok.index)
470	          }
471	        }
472	
473	        const toAssign = Math.min(orphanItems.length, gapCount)
474	        for (let i = 0; i < toAssign; i++) {
475	          const num = gapStart + i
476	          exerciseMatches.push({
477	            index: orphanItems[i],
478	            title: `תרגיל ${num}`,
479	            number: num,
480	            fullMatch: '\\item',
481	          })
482	          allFound.add(num)
483	        }
484	
485	        gapStart = gapEnd
486	      }
487	    }
488	
489	    // Sort by exercise number for consistent display order
490	    exerciseMatches.sort((a, b) => a.number - b.number)
491	
492	    if (exerciseMatches.length === 0) {
493	      // No exercises found — treat entire text as one exercise
494	      exercises.push({
495	        number: 1,
496	        title: 'תרגיל 1',
497	        header: '',
498	        latexContent: runText,
499	        solution: null,
500	        solutionHeader: null,
501	        hasDiagram: hasDiagramCheck(runText),
502	        startIndex: 0,
503	        endIndex: runText.length,
504	      })
505	    } else {
506	      // Sort by text position for correct content boundary slicing
507	      const byPosition = [...exerciseMatches].sort((a, b) => a.index - b.index)
508	
509	      // Process each exercise
510	      for (let i = 0; i < exerciseMatches.length; i++) {
511	        const current = exerciseMatches[i]
512	        // Find the next exercise by text position (not by number) for content boundary
513	        const posIdx = byPosition.indexOf(current)
514	        const nextByPos = posIdx < byPosition.length - 1 ? byPosition[posIdx + 1] : null
515	
516	        // Content starts after the exercise header
517	        const contentStart = current.index + current.fullMatch.length
518	
519	        // Content ends at the EARLIEST of:
520	        //   - a short-answer block for this exercise number
521	        //   - the next exercise boundary (by position)
522	        //   - the solutions section
523	        const shortAnswerBlock = shortAnswerBlocks.get(current.number)
524	        const shortAnswerEnd = shortAnswerBlock?.index ?? Infinity
525	        const nextExerciseEnd = nextByPos?.index ?? Infinity
526	        const contentEnd = Math.min(shortAnswerEnd, nextExerciseEnd, firstSolutionIndex)
527	
528	        const latexContent = runText.slice(contentStart, contentEnd).trim()
529	
530	        // Find matching solution — prefer the longest match when duplicates exist
531	        const solCandidates = solutionMatches.filter((s) => s.number === current.number)
532	        let solution: string | null = null
533	        let solutionHeader: string | null = null
534	        for (const solMatch of solCandidates) {
535	          const solContentStart = solMatch.index + solMatch.fullMatch.length
536	          const nextSol = solutionMatches.find((s) => s.index > solMatch.index)
537	          const solContentEnd = nextSol ? nextSol.index : runText.length
538	          const candidate = runText.slice(solContentStart, solContentEnd).trim()
539	          if (candidate.length > (solution?.length ?? 0)) {
540	            solutionHeader = solMatch.fullMatch
541	            solution = candidate
542	          }
543	        }
544	
545	        // If no formal \section*{פתרון} solution was found but there's a short-answer
546	        // block for this exercise, use the short-answer block content as the solution.
547	        // This gives at least brief answers (e.g. "א. $S = 80$, ב. לא...") instead of
548	        // nothing when the LaTeX has no full solutions section.
549	        if (!solution && shortAnswerBlock) {
550	          const shortStart = shortAnswerBlock.index + shortAnswerBlock.fullMatch.length
551	          // Short-answer block ends at the next exercise, next short-answer block,
552	          // solutions section, or end of text — whichever comes first
553	          const nextShortAnswer = [...shortAnswerBlocks.values()]
554	            .filter((b) => b.index > shortAnswerBlock.index)
555	            .sort((a, b) => a.index - b.index)[0]
556	          const nextExerciseAfter = byPosition.find((e) => e.index > shortAnswerBlock.index)
557	          const shortEnd = Math.min(
558	            nextShortAnswer?.index ?? Infinity,
559	            nextExerciseAfter?.index ?? Infinity,
560	            firstSolutionIndex,
561	          )
562	          const shortContent = runText.slice(shortStart, shortEnd).trim()
563	          if (shortContent) {
564	            solution = shortContent
565	          }
566	        }
567	
568	        exercises.push({
569	          number: current.number,
570	          title: current.title,
571	          header: current.fullMatch,
572	          latexContent,
573	          solution,
574	          solutionHeader,
575	          hasDiagram: hasDiagramCheck(latexContent),
576	          startIndex: current.index,
577	          endIndex: contentEnd,
578	        })
579	      }
580	    }
581	
582	    // For the primary \textbf{תרגיל N} pattern only: dedup by exercise number
583	    // (keeping the longest content variant), then if any exercise in this run
584	    // has a matched solution, drop phantom PRIMARY matches that lack one. Why:
585	    // the page-by-page LLM extraction occasionally emits stray \textbf{תרגיל N}
586	    // headers over answer-summary fragments or sub-item labels (ה. ו.) that
587	    // never get a corresponding \section*{פתרון תרגיל N}. Secondary-detected
588	    // exercises (setCounter / continuation / orphan-fill) are NOT subject to
589	    // this filter — they're real continuations of a numbered list and may
590	    // legitimately lack their own solution header in the source.
591	    let finalExercises = exercises
592	    if (usedPrimaryPattern) {
593	      const byNumber = new Map<number, ParsedExercise>()
594	      for (const ex of exercises) {
595	        const existing = byNumber.get(ex.number)
596	        if (!existing || ex.latexContent.length > existing.latexContent.length) {
597	          byNumber.set(ex.number, ex)
598	        }
599	      }
600	      const dedup = Array.from(byNumber.values())
601	      const anyHasSolution = dedup.some((ex) => ex.solution !== null)
602	      if (anyHasSolution) {
603	        finalExercises = dedup.filter(
604	          (ex) => ex.solution !== null || !primaryNumbers.has(ex.number),
605	        )
606	      } else {
607	        finalExercises = dedup
608	      }
609	    }
610	
611	    segments.push({
612	      exercises: finalExercises,
613	      extractionIndex: runIndex + 1,
614	      originalText: runText,
615	    })
616	  }
617	
618	  return segments
619	}

621	/**
622	 * Reconstruct the full ContextExtractions text from edited segments.
623	 * Rebuilds each run by replacing exercise/solution content while preserving
624	 * the document preamble, headers, and delimiters.
625	 */
626	export function reconstructContextText(segments: ParsedSegment[]): string {
627	  const runs: string[] = []
628	
629	  for (const segment of segments) {
630	    const runText = segment.originalText
631	    const { exercises } = segment
632	
633	    // If only one exercise with no header, the entire run IS the content
634	    if (exercises.length === 1 && !exercises[0].header) {
635	      runs.push(exercises[0].latexContent)
636	      continue
637	    }
638	
639	    // Find preamble (everything before first exercise)
640	    const firstExercise = exercises[0]
641	    const preamble = runText.slice(0, firstExercise.startIndex)
642	
643	    // Rebuild: preamble + exercises + solutions
644	    const parts: string[] = [preamble]
645	
646	    for (const ex of exercises) {
647	      parts.push(ex.header)
648	      parts.push('\n')
649	      parts.push(ex.latexContent)
650	      parts.push('\n\n')
651	    }
652	
653	    // Rebuild solutions section
654	    for (const ex of exercises) {
655	      if (ex.solution !== null && ex.solutionHeader) {
656	        parts.push(ex.solutionHeader)
657	        parts.push('\n')
658	        parts.push(ex.solution)
659	        parts.push('\n\n')
660	      }
661	    }
662	
663	    // Check if there's a \end{document} that should be preserved
664	    if (runText.includes('\\end{document}') && !parts.some((p) => p.includes('\\end{document}'))) {
665	      parts.push('\\end{document}\n')
666	    }
667	
668	    runs.push(parts.join(''))
669	  }
670	
671	  return runs.join('\n\n---\n\n')
672	}

395	        })
396	        const tempContent = tempExercise.content as { blocks?: ContentBlock[] } | null
397	        if (tempContent?.blocks) {
398	          allBlocks.push(...tempContent.blocks)
399	        }
400	      } catch {
401	        reqLogger.warn({ tempId }, 'Could not read temp exercise from AI fallback')
402	      }
403	    }
404	
405	    // Clean up temp exercises — they were scaffolding for this in-place conversion.
406	    for (const tempId of tempIds) {
407	      try {
408	        await req.payload.delete({
409	          collection: 'exercises',
410	          id: tempId,
411	          overrideAccess: true,
412	        })
413	      } catch {
414	        reqLogger.warn({ tempId }, 'Could not delete temp exercise from AI fallback')
415	      }
416	    }
417	
418	    reqLogger.info(
419	      { tempExercises: tempIds.length, harvestedBlocks: allBlocks.length },
420	      'AI fallback produced blocks',
421	    )
422	
423	    return allBlocks.length > 0 ? allBlocks : null
424	  } catch (err) {
425	    reqLogger.error({ err }, 'AI fallback threw unexpectedly')
426	    return null
427	  }

460	/**
461	 * Sanity-check that the script parser produced meaningful output.
462	 * If the total text content of all parsed blocks is tiny compared to the
463	 * source LaTeX, the parser likely dropped most of the content and we should
464	 * fall through to AI instead of persisting garbage.
465	 */
466	function isScriptOutputMeaningful(sourceLatex: string, parsedBlocks: ContentBlock[]): boolean {
467	  const totalContent = parsedBlocks
468	    .map((b) => {
469	      if ('value' in b && typeof b.value === 'string') return b.value
470	      if ('latex' in b && typeof b.latex === 'string') return b.latex
471	      return ''
472	    })
473	    .join('')
474	
475	  // If source is non-trivial but output captured less than 10% of it, it's garbage.
476	  if (sourceLatex.length > 50 && totalContent.length < sourceLatex.length * 0.1) {
477	    return false
478	  }
479	
480	  // If output is under 10 chars total regardless, it's garbage.
481	  if (totalContent.length < 10) {
482	    return false
483	  }
484	
485	  return true
486	}

488	/**
489	 * Split a solution LaTeX string by sub-question labels.
490	 *
491	 * Returns an array of solution parts in document order. Each part starts at
492	 * a label and ends just before the next label.
493	 *
494	 * Modes:
495	 * - 'hebrew': only top-level Hebrew letter labels (א., ב., ג., ...)
496	 * - 'all': both Hebrew letter and parenthetical numeric labels ((1), (2), ...).
497	 *   Empty parent labels (e.g. ג. immediately followed by (1)(2) with no
498	 *   content in between) are filtered out so they don't create empty parts.
499	 */
500	export function splitSolutionByLabels(text: string, mode: 'hebrew' | 'all'): string[] {
501	  const hebrewLetters = 'אבגדהוזחטי'
502	  const labelRegexes: RegExp[] = [new RegExp(`(^|\\n)\\s*([${hebrewLetters}])\\.\\s`, 'g')]
503	  if (mode === 'all') {
504	    labelRegexes.push(/(^|\n)\s*\((\d+)\)\s/g)
505	  }
506	
507	  // Collect all label match positions
508	  const positions: number[] = []
509	  for (const re of labelRegexes) {
510	    let m: RegExpExecArray | null
511	    while ((m = re.exec(text)) !== null) {
512	      // m.index points to the leading char (^ or \n). Skip past the leading
513	      // newline to anchor the part at the actual label character.
514	      positions.push(m.index + (m[1] === '\n' ? 1 : 0))
515	    }
516	  }
517	  positions.sort((a, b) => a - b)
518	  // Deduplicate identical positions
519	  const uniquePositions = positions.filter((p, i) => i === 0 || p !== positions[i - 1])
520	  if (uniquePositions.length === 0) return []
521	
522	  // Build candidate parts: each part runs from this position to the next
523	  // (or to the end of the text)
524	  const candidates: string[] = []
525	  for (let i = 0; i < uniquePositions.length; i++) {
526	    const start = uniquePositions[i]
527	    const end = i + 1 < uniquePositions.length ? uniquePositions[i + 1] : text.length
528	    candidates.push(text.slice(start, end).trim())
529	  }
530	
531	  // Filter out empty parent labels: a label whose content is just the label
532	  // itself (e.g. "ג." with nothing after it before the next label). These
533	  // happen when ג. is just a header before nested (1)(2).
534	  const meaningful = candidates.filter((part) => {
535	    // Strip leading label and check if anything substantial remains
536	    const stripped = part.replace(/^\s*([אבגדהוזחטי]\.|\(\d+\))\s*/, '').trim()
537	    return stripped.length > 0
538	  })
539	
540	  return meaningful
541	}

543	/**
544	 * Attach solution LaTeX content to question blocks' `solution` field.
545	 *
546	 * Strategy (in order of preference):
547	 * 1. Strip the solution header (if present)
548	 * 2. Find question blocks in document order
549	 * 3. If 0 question blocks → nothing to do
550	 * 4. If 1 question block → attach the whole solution to it
551	 * 5. If 2+ question blocks:
552	 *    a. Pass 1: split by Hebrew letter labels only
553	 *    b. Pass 2: split by Hebrew letters + numeric labels
554	 *    c. Pass 3: split by paragraph (double-newline) when no labels found
555	 *    For each pass, do best-effort distribution:
556	 *      - parts == questions → 1:1
557	 *      - parts > questions → first (N-1) get 1:1, last gets all remaining joined
558	 *      - parts < questions → first M questions get parts, rest stay empty
559	 *        (AI auto-fill will catch the empty ones in Phase 6)
560	 *    The first pass to produce any parts wins.
561	 * 6. If no pass produces any parts → fallback: dump on last question block
562	 */
563	export function attachSolutionToBlocks(blocks: ContentBlock[], solutionLatex: string): void {
564	  // Clean solution: strip the header line, keep the content
565	  const lines = solutionLatex.split('\n')
566	  const firstLine = lines.find((l) => l.trim().length > 0) || ''
567	  const contentWithoutHeader = isSolutionHeader(firstLine)
568	    ? lines
569	        .slice(lines.indexOf(firstLine) + 1)
570	        .join('\n')
571	        .trim()
572	    : solutionLatex.trim()
573	
574	  if (!contentWithoutHeader) return
575	
576	  // Find question blocks
577	  const questionIndices: number[] = []
578	  for (let i = 0; i < blocks.length; i++) {
579	    const t = blocks[i].type
580	    if (
581	      t === 'question_free_response' ||
582	      t === 'question_select' ||
583	      t === 'question_table' ||
584	      t === 'question_axis' ||
585	      t === 'question_geometry'
586	    ) {
587	      questionIndices.push(i)
588	    }
589	  }
590	
591	  if (questionIndices.length === 0) return
592	
593	  // Single question: whole solution goes to it
594	  if (questionIndices.length === 1) {
595	    setSolutionOnBlock(blocks[questionIndices[0]], contentWithoutHeader)
596	    return
597	  }
598	
599	  // Multiple questions: try increasingly aggressive splitting strategies.
600	  // The first strategy that produces any parts wins; we then distribute
601	  // best-effort even if counts don't match exactly.
602	  const strategies: (() => string[])[] = [
603	    () => splitSolutionByLabels(contentWithoutHeader, 'hebrew'),
604	    () => splitSolutionByLabels(contentWithoutHeader, 'all'),
605	    () => splitByParagraphs(contentWithoutHeader),
606	  ]
607	
608	  for (const strategy of strategies) {
609	    const parts = strategy()
610	    if (parts.length > 0) {
611	      distributeParts(blocks, questionIndices, parts)
612	      return
613	    }
614	  }
615	
616	  // No strategy produced any parts — dump on last question block as ultimate fallback
617	  setSolutionOnBlock(blocks[questionIndices[questionIndices.length - 1]], contentWithoutHeader)
618	}
619	
620	/**
621	 * Split text by paragraph boundaries (double newlines). Used as a fallback
622	 * when label-based splitting finds no labels.
623	 *
624	 * Returns paragraphs that have substantive content (>10 chars after trim).
625	 */
626	function splitByParagraphs(text: string): string[] {
627	  const paragraphs = text
628	    .split(/\n\s*\n+/)
629	    .map((p) => p.trim())
630	    .filter((p) => p.length > 10)
631	  // Only useful as a strategy if we got more than one paragraph
632	  return paragraphs.length >= 2 ? paragraphs : []
633	}
634	
635	/**
636	 * Distribute solution parts across question blocks with best-effort matching:
637	 * - parts.length === questions.length → 1:1 by position
638	 * - parts.length >  questions.length → first (N-1) get 1:1, last block gets
639	 *   all remaining parts joined (so no content is lost)
640	 * - parts.length <  questions.length → first M questions get a part each,
641	 *   remaining questions stay empty (AI auto-fill in Phase 6 will fill them)
642	 */
643	function distributeParts(blocks: ContentBlock[], questionIndices: number[], parts: string[]): void {
644	  const N = questionIndices.length
645	  const M = parts.length
646	
647	  if (M === N) {
648	    // Perfect 1:1 match
649	    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
650	      setSolutionOnBlock(blocks[questionIndices[i]], parts[i])
651	    }
652	    return
653	  }
654	
655	  if (M > N) {
656	    // More parts than questions: first (N-1) get 1:1, last gets the rest
657	    for (let i = 0; i < N - 1; i++) {
658	      setSolutionOnBlock(blocks[questionIndices[i]], parts[i])
659	    }
660	    const remaining = parts.slice(N - 1).join('\n\n')
661	    setSolutionOnBlock(blocks[questionIndices[N - 1]], remaining)
662	    return
663	  }
664	
665	  // M < N: fewer parts than questions. Assign each part to a question;
666	  // remaining questions stay empty for the AI auto-fill step to handle.
667	  for (let i = 0; i < M; i++) {
668	    setSolutionOnBlock(blocks[questionIndices[i]], parts[i])
669	  }
670	}

701	/**
702	 * Fill missing `hint` and `solution` fields on question blocks using AI generation.
703	 *
704	 * Walks the blocks array and, for each question block that's missing either
705	 * a hint or a solution, calls the same `generateSupport` service the admin
706	 * "Generate with AI" button uses.
707	 *
708	 * Only requests the fields that are missing — if the block already has a
709	 * solution from the LaTeX (Phase 4), we only ask the AI to generate a hint.
710	 *
711	 * Mutates `blocks` in place. Failures are logged and the block is left as-is
712	 * rather than aborting the conversion.
713	 */
714	export async function fillMissingSolutionsWithAI(
715	  blocks: ContentBlock[],
716	  payload: PayloadRequest['payload'],
717	  reqLogger: typeof logger,
718	): Promise<void> {
719	  // For each question block, determine which fields are missing
720	  type Target = {
721	    idx: number
722	    targetFields: ('hints' | 'solution')[]
723	  }
724	  const targets: Target[] = []
725	  for (let i = 0; i < blocks.length; i++) {
726	    if (!isQuestionBlock(blocks[i])) continue
727	    const b = blocks[i] as ContentBlock & { hint?: InlineRichText; solution?: InlineRichText }
728	    const missing: ('hints' | 'solution')[] = []
729	    if (!b.hint?.value) missing.push('hints')
730	    if (!b.solution?.value) missing.push('solution')
731	    if (missing.length > 0) {
732	      targets.push({ idx: i, targetFields: missing })
733	    }
734	  }
735	
736	  if (targets.length === 0) {
737	    return
738	  }
739	
740	  reqLogger.info(
741	    {
742	      blocksToFill: targets.length,
743	      totalBlocks: blocks.length,
744	      breakdown: targets.map((t) => ({ idx: t.idx, fields: t.targetFields })),
745	    },
746	    'Filling missing hints/solutions with AI',
747	  )
748	
749	  // Generate sequentially to avoid overwhelming the LLM API / hitting rate limits.
750	  for (const { idx, targetFields } of targets) {
751	    const block = blocks[idx]
752	    try {
753	      const result = await generateSupport(
754	        {
755	          block,
756	          targetFields,
757	        },
758	        payload,
759	      )
760	
761	      if (result.success && result.data) {
762	        // applyGeneratedSupport handles the immutable update — replace in place
763	        blocks[idx] = applyGeneratedSupport(block, result.data, false)
764	      } else {
765	        reqLogger.warn(
766	          { blockId: block.id, fields: targetFields, error: result.error },
767	          'AI hint/solution generation failed for block',
768	        )
769	      }
770	    } catch (err) {
771	      reqLogger.warn(
772	        {
773	          blockId: block.id,
774	          fields: targetFields,
775	          error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : 'Unknown error',
776	        },
777	        'AI hint/solution generation threw',
778	      )
779	    }
780	  }
781	}

783	/**
784	 * Detect a trailing rich_text block that is actually solution content (e.g.,
785	 * the script parser converted `\section*{פתרון תרגיל N}` into a markdown
786	 * `## פתרון ...` header followed by answers). Such a block ends up rendered
787	 * as a separate "page" to students, mixing the answers into the question flow.
788	 *
789	 * If detected: extract the body (after the header line), attach it to the
790	 * preceding question block's `solution` field (when empty), and remove the
791	 * rich_text block from the array.
792	 *
793	 * Mutates `blocks` in place.
794	 */
795	function rerouteTrailingSolutionRichText(blocks: ContentBlock[], reqLogger: typeof logger): void {
796	  if (blocks.length === 0) return
797	
798	  // Find the last non-empty rich_text block
799	  let trailingIdx = -1
800	  for (let i = blocks.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
801	    const b = blocks[i]
802	    if (b.type === 'rich_text') {
803	      trailingIdx = i
804	      break
805	    }
806	    // Stop if we hit a question block (means there's no trailing rich_text after questions)
807	    if (isQuestionBlock(b)) {
808	      return
809	    }
810	  }
811	
812	  if (trailingIdx < 0) return
813	
814	  const trailing = blocks[trailingIdx] as ContentBlock & { value?: string }
815	  const value = trailing.value || ''
816	  if (!looksLikeSolutionContent(value)) return
817	
818	  // Strip the solution header line and use the rest as the solution body
819	  const body = stripSolutionHeaderLine(value).trim()
820	  if (!body) {
821	    // Header only with no body — just remove the block, nothing to attach
822	    blocks.splice(trailingIdx, 1)
823	    reqLogger.info(
824	      { blockId: trailing.id },
825	      'Removed trailing rich_text solution header (empty body)',
826	    )
827	    return
828	  }
829	
830	  // Find the previous question block (could be at any position before trailingIdx)
831	  let qIdx = -1
832	  for (let i = trailingIdx - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
833	    if (isQuestionBlock(blocks[i])) {
834	      qIdx = i
835	      break
836	    }
837	  }
838	
839	  if (qIdx < 0) {
840	    // No question block to attach to — just remove the trailing solution block
841	    // (it's not useful as a standalone block to students)
842	    blocks.splice(trailingIdx, 1)
843	    reqLogger.info(
844	      { blockId: trailing.id },
845	      'Removed trailing rich_text solution (no preceding question block)',
846	    )
847	    return
848	  }
849	
850	  const qBlock = blocks[qIdx] as ContentBlock & { solution?: InlineRichText }
851	  if (qBlock.solution?.value) {
852	    // Question already has a solution — append rather than overwrite
853	    qBlock.solution = {
854	      type: 'rich_text',
855	      format: 'md-math-v1',
856	      value: `${qBlock.solution.value}\n\n${body}`,
857	      mediaIds: [],
858	    }
859	    reqLogger.info(
860	      { questionBlockId: qBlock.id, removedBlockId: trailing.id },
861	      'Appended trailing solution rich_text to existing question solution',
862	    )
863	  } else {
864	    qBlock.solution = {
865	      type: 'rich_text',
866	      format: 'md-math-v1',
867	      value: body,
868	      mediaIds: [],
869	    }
870	    reqLogger.info(
871	      { questionBlockId: qBlock.id, removedBlockId: trailing.id },
872	      'Moved trailing solution rich_text into question solution',
873	    )
874	  }
875	
876	  // Remove the rich_text block
877	  blocks.splice(trailingIdx, 1)
878	}
879	
880	/**
881	 * Detect whether a rich_text block's value looks like solution content.
882	 * After parsing, `\section*{פתרון}` becomes `## פתרון`, `\textbf{פתרון}`
883	 * becomes `**פתרון**`, etc. We match the post-parse forms.
884	 */
885	export function looksLikeSolutionContent(value: string): boolean {
886	  if (!value) return false
887	  const firstLine =
888	    value
889	      .trim()
890	      .split('\n')
891	      .find((l) => l.trim().length > 0) || ''
892	
893	  // Markdown header from \section*{פתרון ...} or \subsection*{פתרון ...}
894	  if (/^#{1,3}\s*פתרון/.test(firstLine)) return true
895	  if (/^#{1,3}\s*פתרונות/.test(firstLine)) return true
896	  if (/^#{1,3}\s*תשובה\s+סופית/.test(firstLine)) return true
897	
898	  // Bold from \textbf{פתרון תרגיל N:} or \textbf{פתרון שאלה N:}
899	  if (/^\*\*\s*פתרון\s+(?:תרגיל|שאלה)/.test(firstLine)) return true
900	
901	  // Plain prefix (parser may strip formatting in some cases)
902	  if (/^פתרון\s+(?:תרגיל|שאלה)\s+\d+/.test(firstLine)) return true
903	  if (/^פתרונות(\s|$)/.test(firstLine)) return true
904	  if (/^תשובה\s+סופית/.test(firstLine)) return true
905	
906	  return false
907	}
908	
909	/**
910	 * Remove the first non-empty line if it's a solution header marker,
911	 * leaving the body of the solution. Used to clean rich_text content
912	 * before attaching it to a question's `solution` field.
913	 */
914	function stripSolutionHeaderLine(value: string): string {
915	  const lines = value.split('\n')
916	  const firstNonEmptyIdx = lines.findIndex((l) => l.trim().length > 0)
917	  if (firstNonEmptyIdx < 0) return value
918	  const firstLine = lines[firstNonEmptyIdx]
919	  const isHeader =
920	    /^#{1,3}\s*פתרון/.test(firstLine.trim()) ||
921	    /^#{1,3}\s*פתרונות/.test(firstLine.trim()) ||
922	    /^\*\*\s*פתרון/.test(firstLine.trim()) ||
923	    /^פתרון\s+(?:תרגיל|שאלה)/.test(firstLine.trim()) ||
924	    /^פתרונות(\s|$)/.test(firstLine.trim()) ||
925	    /^תשובה\s+סופית/.test(firstLine.trim())
926	  if (!isHeader) return value
927	  return lines.slice(firstNonEmptyIdx + 1).join('\n')
928	}
929	
930	/**
931	 * Remove a trailing rich_text block when all question blocks already have
932	 * solution content populated. This catches redundant duplicates of the
933	 * solution that survived earlier rerouting (e.g., when looksLikeSolutionContent
934	 * didn't match the exact format).
935	 *
936	 * Only removes if:
937	 * - The last block is a rich_text block
938	 * - There are question blocks BEFORE it in the array
939	 * - All question blocks have a non-empty `solution` field
940	 *
941	 * The third condition is the safety net: if any question still lacks a
942	 * solution, we leave the trailing block alone — it may be the only place
943	 * the solution content exists.
944	 */
945	export function removeRedundantTrailingSolution(
946	  blocks: ContentBlock[],
947	  reqLogger: typeof logger,
948	): void {
949	  if (blocks.length === 0) return
950	
951	  const last = blocks[blocks.length - 1]
952	  if (last.type !== 'rich_text') return
953	
954	  // Collect question blocks before the trailing rich_text
955	  const questionBlocks = blocks.slice(0, -1).filter((b) => isQuestionBlock(b))
956	  if (questionBlocks.length === 0) return // nothing to be redundant against
957	
958	  // Check that ALL question blocks have a non-empty solution
959	  const allHaveSolution = questionBlocks.every((b) => {
960	    const qb = b as ContentBlock & { solution?: InlineRichText }
961	    return Boolean(qb.solution?.value)
962	  })
963	
964	  if (!allHaveSolution) {
965	    // At least one question lacks a solution — keep the trailing block
966	    // since it might be the only place that content exists
967	    return
968	  }
969	
970	  // Safe to remove — all questions have their own solutions
971	  blocks.splice(blocks.length - 1, 1)
972	  reqLogger.info(
973	    { removedBlockId: last.id },
974	    'Removed trailing rich_text block (all question blocks have solutions)',
975	  )
976	}
